items.title: A Source of Mysterious Repeating Radio Signals From Space Has Been Identified
items.summary: Researchers say the discovery could be a “Rosetta stone” for cosmic signals.

items.title: Meta Pauses Employee-Tracking Program Following Internal Data Leak
items.summary: The move comes after the company left potentially sensitive data from the initiative exposed internally.

items.title: Meta Exposed Data Internally From Its Controversial Employee-Tracking Program
items.summary: Employees had previously raised concerns about the initiative, which involves collecting workers’ keystroke data to train AI models.

items.title: Tesla pushes back on Autopilot narrative after fatal Texas crash
items.summary: Whether the Autopilot system was truly active, overridden, or malfunctioning likely won't be resolved until investigators finish combing through the vehicle's data logs.

items.title: Shareholders sue Uber’s board over sexual assaults, other incidents
items.summary: The lawsuit, led by a Detroit pension fund, alleges Uber's board and management has cut too many compliance corners, resulting in thousands of lawsuits.

items.title: The AI world is getting ‘loopy’
items.summary: The loop takes agentic AI a step further by authorizing a swarm of agents to work continuously in the background, endlessly.

items.title: Vance says Iran will allow nuclear inspectors back into the country
items.summary: The US vice-president says a great deal of progress has been made after the first round of talks between the US and Iran.

items.title: At least 13 killed and dozens injured after Qatar gas explosion
items.summary: The city's main liquified natural gas (LNG) processing site suffered a "technical accident" in the Ras Laffan industrial zone.

items.title: Myanmar army killed over 700 civilians in six months, UN says
items.summary: The new UN report says the 702 civilian deaths over six months last year included 153 children.

items.title: Can anyone join Musk in the trillionaire club? Zuckerberg has best shot, according to prediction markets
items.summary: The Meta CEO is seen as next to join the Tesla and SpaceX CEO. Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang is seen as the second-most-likely.

items.title: Alan Greenspan, former chairman of the Fed, dies at age 100
items.summary: Alan Greenspan presided over the Federal Reserve for 19 years under four presidents and mastered the art of obfuscation known as Fedspeak.

items.title: 'Regime change but in a velvet glove': How Kevin Warsh has set out to remake the Fed
items.summary: The first big announced changes point toward a quiet revolution, with task forces set up to rethink virtually everything done at the Fed.

items.title: Stocks Sink in Broad AI Rout Sparked by China's DeepSeek
items.summary: U.S. stocks were mostly lower, with the Nasdaq leading declines as makers of AI infrastructure suffered steep falls, many in the double digits. Nvidia was down 16%.

items.title: Comex Gold, Silver Settle Lower
items.summary: Gold settled 1.4% lower, down two of the past three sessions, and silver fell 2.5%, down three of the past four sessions.

items.title: DeepSeek Won't Sink U.S. AI Titans
items.summary: Panic fueling the selloff of Nvidia, Broadcom and other tech giants is overblown.

items.title: Senate overwhelmingly passes sweeping bipartisan housing affordability bill
items.summary: The Senate on Monday overwhelmingly passed a bipartisan bill aimed at lowering housing costs, sending the legislation to the House. The rare show of near-unanimous support comes as lawmakers hustle to score wins on affordability that they can trumpet back home during a midterm campaign cycle that has focused heavily on rising costs. The bill,…

items.title: Maher mocks Obama presidential library: ‘Who’s going to go to this?’
items.summary: Comedian Bill Maher during his latest show mocked the Obama Presidential Center, which opened in Chicago last week. “It looks like something aliens built in Dubai,” Maher remarked on the Friday episode of HBO’s “Real Time” while talking with Rep. Ro Khanna (D-Calif.) and Politico journalist Jonathan Martin. “It cost $850 million,” he added later.…

items.title: Carville encourages leaks from Trump White House staffers
items.summary: Democratic strategist James Carville on Sunday encouraged Trump administration staffers to start leaking details about closed door dealings within the White House in an effort to save their careers.  Carville has shared numerous predictions that President Trump and Republicans will lose power after the midterm elections, leading to intense backlash for individuals closely associated with…

items.title: Fast-food seafood chain closed over 900 stores, mounts comeback
items.summary: Fast-food seafood chain closed over 900 stores, mounts comeback

items.title: Michael Burry sees a $3 trillion problem with SpaceX
items.summary: Michael Burry sees a $3 trillion problem with SpaceX

items.title: Is Absci Corporation (ABSI) A Good Stock To Buy Now?
items.summary: Is Absci Corporation (ABSI) A Good Stock To Buy Now?

items.title: A common vitamin could help fight one of the deadliest brain cancers
items.summary: A clinical trial is exploring whether high doses of vitamin B3 could give patients with glioblastoma a better chance against the aggressive brain cancer. Scientists found that niacin may help revive immune cells that tumors shut down, allowing them to attack cancer more effectively. Early results have been promising, with patients showing significantly better progression-free survival than expected.

items.title: Butterfly that barely ages could help unlock longevity secrets
items.summary: Scientists discovered that Heliconius butterflies have evolved an extraordinary lifespan, living several times longer than closely related species. Even more surprising, some show little sign of physical decline as they age. Their unusual pollen-feeding lifestyle may play a role, but the research suggests deeper evolutionary changes are also helping them stay healthy for longer.

items.title: Future astronauts could walk across rocks from deep inside the Moon
items.summary: A colossal ancient collision may have left some of the Moon’s deepest secrets surprisingly close to future Artemis landing sites. By recreating the impact that formed the giant South Pole-Aitken basin—the Moon’s largest and oldest crater—scientists found that a low-angle strike from a large, iron-cored object blasted material from deep inside the Moon, including mantle rocks.

items.title: The New World screwworm has returned to the U.S. Now what?
items.summary: At least a dozen animals have been found with the flesh-eating maggots. It could take more than a year to eradicate the parasite again, experts warn.

items.title: Sea cucumbers harbor ‘zombie’ tissues that won’t die
items.summary: Detached tissues from the sea cucumber's tube feet and feeding tentacles survived for more than three years, a find that could shape the study of aging.

items.title: A tropical permafrost layer in Peru may be one of the world’s largest
items.summary: Vast permafrost beneath the upper slopes of Peru’s tallest volcano could become a regional water source as glaciers in the Andes retreat.
